content: ICD therapy for SCD prevention: Secondary prevention (survivors of cardiac arrest or sustained VT with hemodynamic compromise)—Class I indication unless reversible cause identified. Primary prevention (prevent first event): LVEF ≤35% despite ≥3 months GDMT with NYHA class II-III symptoms (ischemic or nonischemic cardiomyopathy); LVEF ≤30% with NYHA class I (ischemic); post-MI ≥40 days (earlier placement not beneficial—DINAMIT trial showed harm); nonischemic dilated cardiomyopathy (DEFINITE, SCD-HeFT trials). Timing critical: wait ≥40 days post-MI, ≥90 days post-revascularization, ≥3 months on GDMT to allow for potential EF recovery. Device types: transvenous ICD (standard), subcutaneous ICD (no intravascular leads—reduces lead complications, no pacing capability except transient post-shock), CRT-D (if QRS ≥150ms with LBBB and EF ≤35%). Complications: inappropriate shocks (SVT, lead failure), infections, lead fractures, psychological impact. Shared decision-making essential, especially in elderly/comorbid patients.
